---
title: "Introduction"
subtitle: "Project Framing and Literature Review"

format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: true
---

## Introduction & Motivation

::: {.callout-note}
### Lab Task 1: The "Why"
Delete this blue box and replace it with 2–3 paragraphs explaining the real-world context of your topic. Why does this problem matter? Who is affected? What real-world impact does solving this problem achieve?
:::

*Start typing your project introduction here...*

---

## Official Research Questions

Your project must be guided by clear, measurable data science inquiries. Use the format below to define your scope.

* **Primary Research Question (RQ1):** *e.g., To what extent can historical local weather patterns predict weekend public transit delays in major urban centers?*
* **Secondary Research Question (RQ2):** *e.g., Does this predictive relationship hold true across different socio-economic neighborhoods within the city limits?*

---

## Initial Literature Review

::: {.callout-tip}
### Academic Scaffolding
Find at least two academic papers, industry reports, or documentation sources relevant to your domain. Summarize their findings below and explain how your specific project builds upon or differs from their methodology.
:::

### Prior Work 1: [Title of Paper/Article Here]
* **Source/Authors:** 
* **Core Finding:** 
* **How it informs our project:** 

### Prior Work 2: [Title of Paper/Article Here]
* **Source/Authors:** 
* **Core Finding:** 
* **How it informs our project:** 

---

## Data Source Orientation

Briefly document the raw data you intend to use to answer your research questions.

| Data Asset Name  | Source Agency / URL | Format (.csv, API, SQL) | Row/Column Count (Est.) |
|----------------- |---------------------|-------------------------|-------------------------|
| *Example Dataset*| *City Data Portal*  | *.csv*                  | *~50,000 x 12*          |
|                  |                     |                         |                         |
